purpose: prove that the former Light/Heavy implementations are one ABHSS algorithm whose frozen configurations differ only by safe additions or same-role replacements, without exactness or solver-time regression

policy.scope: 2026-07-23 original single-entry refactor gate; the 2026-07-24 clarification gate has its own policy block
policy.comparison_unit: sum of solver-only query time over each predeclared panel and fixed configuration
policy.rerun_rule: if one panel exceeds 1.03, alternate old/new at least three times and compare medians

allowed_relations: safe addition with a correctness certificate
replacement of the same logical responsibility by a structurally or functionally corresponding realization
relation_contract.forbidden_relation: a Base-only logical state family or stage with no Enhanced counterpart or same-role replacement

same_role_replacements.group_distance: Base canonical-SPT cutoff plus bounded GroupRow value/IsExact oracle -> Enhanced complete-potential GroupRow value/IsExact oracle without cutoff construction
same_role_replacements.ordinary_future: Base pre-ordinary A1 cone -> Enhanced directed-cut potential
same_role_replacements.upper_witness: Base root-path tree plus pre-D witness-DP evaluation -> Enhanced primal upper plus dual-primal tree as the pre-D/periodic witness realization; common root-star and root-path-union uppers remain shared, and facility is separately classified as a safe addition
same_role_replacements.anchored_singleton_A1: The balanced completion grid has logical layers 1..q where q=max(0,floor(g/2)-1). Base realizes every layer in that grid with forward A and, when layer 1 belongs to the grid, schedules that standard A1 row before D so it can also serve as the ordinary future. Enhanced realizes the low prefix with forward A and the remaining suffix with H. Layer membership, never an empirical group-count threshold, determines the realization; every materialized row is built at most once
same_role_replacements.high_completion: Base forward high A -> Enhanced low A plus adjoint high H

relation_contract.regression_assertion: abhss_configuration_exactness checks the added-operation subset relation, every replacement realization, and exact one-realization coverage of every required anchored layer for group counts 0..16

inclusion_clarification_gate_20260724.status: SUPERSEDED for the current A1 schedule by structural_a1_schedule_gate_20260724; retained only as historical evidence for the earlier naming-only clarification

non_regression_policy.short_panel_rule: when one old/new pair exceeds 1.03, alternate at least three times and compare medians

timed_candidate_binary.semantic_change: renames EarlyAnchor as the pre-ordinary view of the common A1 layer and adds an auditable add-or-replace configuration profile; the accepted hot solver recurrence is unchanged

final_validation_binary.post_timing_changes: Chinese comments and documentation precision for the then-current selective A1 schedule only; no executable statement, branch, data layout or recurrence changed in that historical candidate

rejected_design.description: force Base pre-ordinary A1 through the generic forward-layer kernel
rejected_design.panel: GPU4GST_Musae cross_g6, five queries, Base; weights and state semantics checked during the trial
rejected_design.decision: rejected because it violated the 3% non-regression threshold despite discovering fewer states

accepted_panels.comparison: seven alternating old/new runs per configuration; compare medians
three alternating old/new runs per configuration; compare medians
one old/new run per configuration; below the preregistered 1.03 rerun trigger
one old/new large-graph run per configuration
one old/new large-graph run per configuration

structural_a1_schedule_gate_20260724.supersedes: inclusion_clarification_gate_20260724 for the current executable A1 scheduling contract

contract.logical_grid: q=max(0,floor(g/2)-1) is the largest anchored-mask cardinality required by the balanced exact-completion recurrence; the required positive layers are exactly 1..q
contract.base: Base realizes the whole required grid with forward A. If A1 is a member of 1..q, the same standard row is generated before ordinary D, reused through a read-only cone-future view, and then ownership-transferred to the common forward kernel without a second closure or a second state count
contract.directed_cut_only: The directed-cut potential replaces the ordinary-future responsibility; the common full forward-A kernel realizes every required anchored layer
contract.enhanced: The directed-cut potential replaces the ordinary-future responsibility. With ell=floor(q/2), common forward A realizes 1..ell and adjoint H realizes the complementary high-layer responsibility ell+1..q
contract.forbidden_dispatch: No graph name, query identity, row density, elapsed time, measured performance, or empirical condition of the form g compared with a tuned constant selects the A1 path
contract.configuration_freeze: The enhancement mask is fixed before the query starts and cannot change during the query

non_regression_policy.comparison_unit: sum of solver-only per-query time over a fixed panel; graph and query loading are excluded
non_regression_policy.short_panel_rule: use at least three alternating runs and compare medians
non_regression_policy.large_graph_rule: one old/new run is an additional direction and memory check, not a substitute for the repeated small/medium panels
non_regression_policy.state_count_rule: state counts may change when A1 is scheduled before D because a different incumbent is visible at discovery time; exact weights must match and the work change must be disclosed

implementation_changes: replace the empirical A1 group-count guard with the logical anchored-grid schedule
move Base's already-existing initial root-path witness evaluation before every A/D state so the newly uniform A1 schedule sees the tighter incumbent
cache the top-two A1 payload locators losslessly in one lazily touched 64-bit item per queried vertex and release the cache after ordinary D
expose and regression-test the safe-addition or same-role-replacement ConfigurationProfile

final_validation_binary.post_timing_changes: move the accepted layer-plan formula and predicates into the shared header so the regression test can inspect the executable contract, and replace forward.cpp's direct half<2 test with the schedule's complete_implicit_anchor bit. The latter distinguishes an empty complete logical grid from an empty Enhanced low prefix with a nonempty H suffix. No recurrence, pruning expression, row layout, A1 cache, or completion layer changed.

final_hash_known_optimum_check.result: Base 11/11 and Enhanced 11/11 match the g=11..16 known optima

final_documented_binary.change_after_interleaved_timing: rename the AddedOperation identifier PrimalFacilityUpperBound to FacilityUpperBound while preserving its underlying bit value. This removes a documentation-category ambiguity: primal upper/witness is the same-role replacement for Base root-path upper/witness, while facility is the safe addition. No executable condition, arithmetic expression, data layout, enum value, or recurrence changed.

release_binary.change_after_documentation_validation: the final top-level A/H dispatch reads completion_schedule.uses_adjoint instead of re-reading the equivalent frozen option bit. The tested ConfigurationProfile and layer schedule now form the single dispatch chain; no layer boundary, arithmetic, row, or loop changed.

repository_audit_rebuild.change_after_release_binary: repository-wide paper-method audit tightened comments and documentation, added unknown-enhancement-bit rejection to the existing configuration regression, and corrected the CMake target-role comments. No ABHSS executable condition, arithmetic expression, data layout, recurrence, or command-line behavior changed
repository_audit_rebuild.windows_hash_note: the MinGW PE contains a link timestamp and debug line sections, so this raw whole-file SHA-256 is an artifact identity rather than a reproducible source identity; the current artifact is recorded instead of reusing the earlier release hash
repository_audit_rebuild.performance_evidence: the release_binary performance panels remain the applicable executable-path evidence because the audit changed no solver statement; no new performance number is inferred from the changed whole-file PE hash

correctness.independent_random_exact_dp: 144 deterministic connected cases, all three legal configurations, g=2..10
correctness.steinlib_known_optima: Base 11/11 and Enhanced 11/11 on WRP instances with g=11..16 after the final build

performance_panels.purpose: stress the formerly deferred A1 case with the complete 300-query author workload
performance_panels.comparison: three runs per binary; compare medians
performance_panels.state_change_explanation: A1 is now generated before D for every query whose logical grid contains that layer; the old path generated it only after D and therefore saw a later, tighter incumbent. The full workload remains inside the preregistered time gate despite the disclosed extra discoveries.

performance_panels.purpose: repeat the existing five-query Base regression panel after the structural schedule change
performance_panels.comparison: three final-candidate runs versus the frozen seven-run old median

performance_panels.purpose: verify that the Enhanced path, whose state work is unchanged, does not regress
performance_panels.comparison: three alternating old/new runs; compare medians

performance_panels.purpose: large-graph solver-time and query-memory direction check
multi-million-vertex sparse-query time and memory direction check

rejected_variants: unconditionally moving A1 before D without a payload-locator cache exceeded the time gate
delaying A1 until after D size 2 improved one panel but regressed another and reintroduced a schedule not derived from the recurrence
caching two doubles per vertex improved lookup time but inflated large-graph RSS
the accepted packed-locator cache preserves the original double payload and the large-graph memory direction

build_identity.optimization: O2 plus the common Release IPO target property

final_validation_binary.name: abhss.exe
final_validation_binary.post_timing_changes: Chinese comments, the graph-I/O regression target/fixture, and removal of one unused include only; no ABHSS control-flow, data-layout, or graph-loader statement changed

correctness.add_or_replace_profile: PASS: added-operation subset relation and all four same-role realizations asserted

graph_loading.implementation: 8 MiB buffered numeric scan; first pass stores edges and counts exact degrees; second pass reserves once and builds bidirectional adjacency in original order

old_process_start_to_ready_seconds.resolution: approximately one second, from process StartTime to the old header timestamp

orkut.conclusion: roughly 18x to 22x lower load latency across measured runs, with lower loaded RSS and an independently passing solver-time gate

old_graph_load_seconds_approximate.derivation: external process wall time minus recorded solver query time; includes subsecond startup/output overhead

reddit.conclusion: approximately 16x lower load latency, with both solver-time panels passing
